dimension_notes.naming_quality: Consistent snake_case naming in Rust modules. Some test files use unclear names like 'test_helpers.rs' for a 1642-line file.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Core business logic is readable. Some boilerplate duplication in service management (openrc.rs).
dimension_notes.type_safety: Strong use of Rust's type system. 67 Result type usages indicate good error handling patterns.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: Functions generally have clear contracts. One returns_null violation in rl_engine/
dimension_notes.error_consistency: Consistent Result<T, E> usage across modules. Single returns_null exception in rl_engine/
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: 20 indirection hotspots with chain depths up to 3. Some unnecessary abstraction layers.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: error.rs has 31% comment ratio (4x codebase avg) suggesting AI-generated over-documentation.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: installer package at 74.2% of codebase is monolithic. Main.rs at 3724 LOC needs decomposition.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: 16 files with wide param bags. config_bag_mentions up to 114 in main.rs.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Complexity hotspot in main.rs (score 1180). Multiple files with high cyclomatic complexity.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: 30 orphaned files with zero importers. Significant dead code or missing entry point declarations.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: Reasonable initialization patterns. No major coupling issues detected.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: 8+ unused imports in main.rs including wildcard imports. Test files have 13+ unused imports.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Dependencies appear well-managed. No circular dependency indicators.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Zero test coverage on critical production files including safety.rs (543 LOC security module).
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: APIs are consistent within modules. Cross-module API coherence needs improvement.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: No authorization issues detected in blind packet analysis.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: No obvious migration markers detected. Codebase appears stable.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Two packages dominate: installer (74.2%) and ai-agents (25.8%). Disproportionate distribution.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: test_helpers.rs has 43 disconnected function clusters indicating mixed responsibilities.

issues.summary: 30 files marked orphaned with zero importers, indicating massive dead code or missing entry point declarations
evidence: Blind packet dead_code section shows 30 orphaned files totaling 8,877 LOC
installer/src/main.rs (3724 LOC) marked orphaned despite being CLI entry point
ai-agents/portcl/src/actions/safety.rs (543 LOC) orphaned with zero importers
issues.suggestion: Add #[bin] or main() entry point markers to CLI entry points. Review if orphaned library modules are actually used via dynamic dispatch or should be removed.

issues.summary: installer package consumes 74.2% of total codebase LOC with single 3724-line main.rs file
evidence: Package size census shows installer at 74.2% of total LOC (3725 lines)
ai-agents package at only 25.8% despite containing multiple sub-projects
Complexity score 1180 on main.rs indicates architectural decomposition failure
issues.suggestion: Decompose installer into domain modules: disk_config/, partitioning/, bootloader/, user_setup/, package_selection/. Extract shared logic to installer-core crate.

issues.summary: Multiple high-complexity production files have zero test coverage
evidence: Signal density shows test_coverage detector on 20 files
installer/src/main.rs: 'Untested critical module (3724 LOC, 0 importers) — high blast radius'
Testing section shows 38 total test files but critical_untested is empty (likely data issue)
issues.suggestion: Prioritize tests for: safety.rs (security-critical), rl_engine/ (algorithm correctness), installer main.rs integration tests. Target 70% coverage on production code.

issues.summary: Multiple files exceed healthy complexity thresholds with cyclomatic complexity indicators
evidence: Complexity hotspots: main.rs (1180), validation.rs (108), portage_actions.rs (100)
Indirection hotspots show continual.rs with max_chain_depth 3 and 47 chains
Large file distribution shows p90 at 3724 LOC, p99 at 3724 LOC
issues.suggestion: Extract functions from main.rs into focused modules. Reduce chain depth in continual.rs by introducing intermediate types or reducing nesting.

issues.summary: 20 files flagged as indirection hotspots with chain depths up to 3 and high chain counts
evidence: continual.rs: max_chain_depth=3, chain_count=47
btrmind/main.rs: max_chain_depth=3, chain_count=22
config/validation.rs: max_chain_depth=3, chain_count=22
Abstraction summary: indirection_hotspot_count=20
issues.suggestion: Reduce method chaining by introducing intermediate variables or helper functions. Flatten builder patterns where chain depth exceeds 2.

issues.summary: Two packages dominate codebase with installer at 74.2% and ai-agents at 25.8%
evidence: Package size census shows both packages marked as 'disproportionate: true'
installer consumes 3725 LOC vs ai-agents at 1295 LOC
No other packages in census indicating flat or missing intermediate structure
issues.suggestion: Split installer into core, cli, and domain-specific crates. Group ai-agents subprojects under a workspace with shared utilities.

issues.summary: Consistent Result type usage but one returns_null violation detected
evidence: Error strategy by directory shows Result type used 67 times across modules
rl_engine/ directory has 'returns_null: 1' alongside 'result_type: 114'
This indicates one function returning null/Option in a Result-heavy codebase
issues.suggestion: Find and convert the single returns_null function to return Result<T, E> for consistency with rest of codebase.

issues.summary: One file has 31% comment ratio vs codebase average of 7.8%, suggesting over-documentation or AI-generated boilerplate
evidence: AI debt signals: error.rs has comment_ratio 0.31
Codebase average comment_ratio: 0.078
4x higher comment ratio indicates potential restating comments or defensive documentation
issues.suggestion: Review error.rs for restating comments that echo code without adding insight. Keep WHY comments, remove WHAT comments.

issues.summary: test_helpers.rs has 43 disconnected function clusters indicating mixed responsibilities in test utilities
evidence: Signal density shows '43 disconnected function clusters (73 functions) — likely mixed responsibilities'
File is 1642 LOC in test fixtures
13 unused imports suggesting dead test helper code
issues.suggestion: Split test_helpers.rs into focused modules: fixture_builders/, assertion_helpers/, mock_factories/. Remove unused imports.

issues.summary: 16 files have config_bag_mentions indicating potential parameter sprawl
evidence: installer/main.rs: config_bag_mentions=114
btrmind/main.rs: config_bag_mentions=39
btrmind/config.rs: config_bag_mentions=37
Abstraction summary: wide_param_bag_count=16
issues.suggestion: Group related config parameters into typed structs. Use builder pattern for complex configuration objects.

issues.summary: Multiple files have unused imports including wildcard imports (*)
evidence: main.rs has 8 unused import warnings including 'unused: Unused import: *'
test_helpers.rs has 13 unused import warnings with multiple wildcard imports
test_models.rs, test_config.rs also show wildcard unused imports
issues.suggestion: Run cargo fix --allow-dirty to auto-remove unused imports. Replace wildcard imports with explicit item lists.

issues.summary: openrc.rs flagged for multiple boilerplate duplication patterns
evidence: Duplicate clusters show openrc.rs appears 7 times across exact, near-duplicate, and boilerplate_duplication kinds
portage.rs also appears in boilerplate_duplication cluster
openrc.rs has 5 different detectors flagging it
issues.suggestion: Extract common service management patterns into a shared trait or base implementation. Reduce boilerplate between OpenRC and systemd implementations.

issues.summary: API patterns vary between packages, reducing cross-module usability
evidence: 30 orphaned files suggest incomplete API exports
Different error handling patterns between installer and ai-agents
No shared API conventions documented
issues.suggestion: Establish API design guidelines. Create shared result types and error handling patterns. Document public API surface for each crate.
